content:
Recently, two streams were created in parallel for the event ACT 2020. Ultimately, one of the streams was designated obsolete, and @Paolo Perrone kindly moved all of the topics in that stream to the other one.
Unfortunately, the stream that remains has a subscriber count of only 61 at the time of writing. While it is possible to "include all members" at the time a stream is created, I cannot find a way of adding a large number of members to that stream at once. Is this possible? If not, then if/when this is added as a feature, might it be possible to add all members who have not already deliberately unsubscribed from a given stream?
What happens if you try renaming one stream to the other? Does that work?

[Mod] Morgan Rogers said:

Recently, two streams were created in parallel for the event ACT 2020. Ultimately, one of the streams was designated obsolete, and Paolo Perrone kindly moved all of the topics in that stream to the other one.
Unfortunately, the stream that remains has a subscriber count of only 61 at the time of writing. While it is possible to "include all members" at the time a stream is created, I cannot find a way of adding a large number of members to that stream at once. Is this possible? If not, then if/when this is added as a feature, might it be possible to add all members who have not already deliberately unsubscribed from a given stream?

I wanted to ask the very same :)
The easy time to add everyone to a stream is when it's created. :concerned:

I don't see any way to add everyone to the ACT 2020 stream now. I can just add individuals one at a time.

So, anyone who wants to partake of ACT 2020 should go to #ACT 2020 and "subscribe".
@Paolo Perrone - is it okay if I take all the streams called "ACT2020 (please use other stream)" and delete them? There's no point having a stream that we're not supposed to use.
Personally I think it's bad etiquette to automatically subscribe everyone to a stream, I've unsubscribed to a few that I got put in automatically

John Baez said:

Paolo Perrone - is it okay if I take all the streams called "ACT2020 (please use other stream)" and delete them? There's no point having a stream that we're not supposed to use.

Yep, we should delete that stream. I think it's better to wait until tomorrow, so people that have been recently active can transition.

Jules Hedges said:

Personally I think it's bad etiquette to automatically subscribe everyone to a stream, I've unsubscribed to a few that I got put in automatically

It's very easy to unsubscribe from a stream, but it's far easier to miss that a new stream has been created. I think that's the line of thinking behind preferring to subscribe people.
I think it's good etiquette to automatically subscribe everyone to a stream, for the reasons Nathanael just mentioned.

If we get to the point where people are creating new streams every day that could change. But clearly the default should be that people get subscribed to ACT2020. Unfortunately whoever created that stream didn't (or couldn't?) do it.
